title: Americans still opt for print books over digital or audio versions; few are in book clubs
summary_en: Most U.S. adults read books, with print still favored over digital formats, though e-book and audiobook use has grown. Habits vary by demographics and few join book clubs.
title_cn: 美国人仍更青睐纸质书而非电子书或有声读物 加入读书俱乐部者占比极低

title: How often does the Supreme Court overturn its own decisions?
summary_en: Fewer than 1% of Supreme Court cases have overturned an older decision. More than half of these cases concerned economic activity or criminal procedure.
title_cn: 最高法院推翻自身判决的频次是多少

title: Video: Can polls tell us who will win on Election Day?
summary_en: We often hear a candidate is leading, trailing, or the race is tied. This is based on “horse race” polling, which estimates how much support candidates have, based on how people answer surveys leading up to an election. But accurately measuring the horse race is wicked hard for a few reasons.
title_cn: 视频：民意调查能否预测选举日获胜方？

title: Negative views of Israel, Netanyahu continue to rise among Americans – especially young people
summary_en: Views of Israel and Benjamin Netanyahu have grown more negative among U.S. adults, with majorities under 50 in both parties rating them poorly.
title_cn: 美国人尤其是年轻群体对以色列、内塔尼亚胡的负面看法持续攀升

title: Gas Prices Are Americans’ Top Concern in Iran War
summary_en: 69% of Americans are worried about higher gas prices due to the Iran war, and 45% say the U.S. is not doing enough to avoid civilian causalities.
title_cn: 汽油价格是伊朗战争期间美国人的首要关切

title: Where Do Americans Get Health Information, and What Do They Trust?
summary_en: Health care providers are a key source for health information. Smaller shares get health information from social media and AI.
title_cn: 美国人健康信息获取渠道及信任情况

title: About half of Americans with siblings are close to at least one of them
summary_en: About half of U.S. adults with a sibling are very or extremely close to at least one, with closeness and support varying by demographics.
title_cn: 约半数有兄弟姐妹的美国人与至少一名兄弟姐妹关系亲密
